details.llm_camera_api: 0/5 — The provided document contains no description of any camera pose setting interface in Robocasa/Robosuite/MuJoCo. It does not mention setting camera position/orientation, does not reference MuJoCo camera fields (e.g., cam_pos, cam_mat0), and provides no explanation of coordinate frame transforms (robot base frame to world frame).
details.llm_offscreen: 0/3 — No offscreen rendering workflow is described. There is no mention of sim.render, env.render, renderers, camera_name usage, image retrieval, or any offscreen rendering configuration.
details.llm_robomimic: 0/4 — No Robomimic integration is discussed. The document does not explain how to access the underlying robosuite environment from Robomimic (e.g., env.env or wrappers), and provides no example code for invoking camera interfaces through Robomimic.
details.llm_notes: 0/3 — No technical caveats are covered. There is no discussion of coordinate frame conventions (base vs world), quaternion ordering (w,x,y,z), rotation representations, or limitations/constraints around updating the same camera pose each timestep.

import robosuite
import numpy as np

env = robosuite.make(
    'Lift',
    robots='Panda',
    has_renderer=False,
    has_offscreen_renderer=True,
    use_camera_obs=True,
    camera_names='agentview',
    camera_heights=256,
    camera_widths=256,
)
obs = env.reset()

sim = env.sim

# Get camera info
cam_id = sim.model.camera_name2id('agentview')
print(f"Camera ID: {cam_id}")
print(f"model.cam_pos[{cam_id}]: {sim.model.cam_pos[cam_id]}")

import mujoco
# Camera modes in MuJoCo:
# 0 = mjCAMFIXED - camera is fixed to its parent body
# 1 = mjCAMTRACK - camera tracks a target body
# 2 = mjCAMTRACKCOM - camera tracks center of mass
# 3 = mjCAMTARGETBODY - camera targets a body
# 4 = mjCAMTARGETBODYCOM - camera targets body COM
print("mjCAMLIGHT_FIXED:", mujoco.mjtCamLight.mjCAMLIGHT_FIXED.value)

"""
Robocasa / Robosuite Camera Movement — Dynamic Camera Pose Control

A minimal, portable solution for dynamically setting camera poses at each
timestep in Robocasa/Robosuite (MuJoCo-based) simulation environments.

Camera poses are expressed in the **robot base frame** so they align
naturally with manipulation data collected via Robomimic.
